items.description: Small irregular tear with dark edge loss and paper protrusion at top left corner.
items.scholarly_note: Typical handling wear at outer corner; no text affected.

items.description: Extensive tear, crumpling and folding of paper at bottom left corner with visible loss and creased overlap.
items.scholarly_note: Significant corner damage from use or storage; text area unaffected.

items.description: Worn, slightly irregular left margin with two small dark spots and possible binding puncture marks.
items.scholarly_note: Consistent with manuscript binding and repeated opening.

items.description: Tiny isolated dark speck in the central-left area of the page.
items.scholarly_note: Minor flyspeck or surface contaminant; no paleographic value.

items.description: Faint horizontal crease line running across upper portion of the folio.
items.scholarly_note: Deformation from age, storage, or handling; paper remains largely flat.

items.description: Additional faint horizontal creases visible toward the bottom of the blank surface.
items.scholarly_note: General paper stress marks with no associated markings.

items.description: Slight surface abrasion and minor discoloration along the binding edge.
items.scholarly_note: Common in heavily consulted codices; superficial only.

scratches: No pen trials, doodles, or deliberate scratches present.
No marginalia, corrections, or annotations of any kind observed.
damage_notes: Top left corner: small tear with edge loss and dark staining.
Bottom left corner: major tear, crumpling and paper loss with folded overlap.
Left edge: wear with small dark marks and possible sewing punctures.
Surface: multiple faint horizontal creases across the folio.
No foxing, water stains, or significant discoloration visible beyond localized edge wear.
summary: Page 2 is a blank folio. No text, marginalia, doodles, pen trials, or corrections. All features are physical: torn top-left and badly damaged bottom-left corners, left-edge binding wear with punctures, faint horizontal creases, and minor surface specks. Damage is confined to margins and consistent with age and handling of the manuscript.
